food_subgroup: Coffee-based beverages with dairy and confectionery additions

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for iced coconut cafe latte with condensed milk and coffee jelly. FOODON_03301737 (coffee beverage) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Coffee and coffee substitutes) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for this composite iced coconut latte with coffee jelly preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 (Coffee substance) is the closest available anchor. No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for this composite beverage.

preparation_method: coffee jelly made by setting brewed coffee with gelatin or agar
main_ingredients: coffee jelly (brewed coffee, gelatin or agar, sugar)
allergens: milk (condensed milk, may contain dairy in coconut milk products)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one cup or glass of 350 g, representing a typical cafe-style iced latte portion as served in Singapore coffee shops.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: coconut milk (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #12117), brewed espresso (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14209), sweetened condensed milk (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #01077), coffee jelly (estimated from brewed coffee and gelatin components, USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14209 and #19177)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for iced coconut cafe latte with condensed milk and coffee jelly. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted component blend: approximately 40% coconut milk, 30% brewed espresso, 20% condensed milk, and 10% coffee jelly (by weight before ice dilution), using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for each component. Per-100g values reflect the blended beverage before ice dilution. Confidence is medium due to estimation from components.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk and dairy-based beverages and analogous estimate for coconut milk coffee beverage; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened condensed milk (GI ~61) and coconut milk-based beverages (GI ~40-50 range); composite estimate weighted toward low GI due to fat content of coconut milk attenuating glucose response.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for iced coconut cafe latte with condensed milk and coffee jelly. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on: (1) condensed milk GI approximately 61 per University of Sydney GI Database; (2) coconut milk fat content substantially attenuates glycemic response, pulling composite GI lower; (3) coffee has negligible glycemic contribution; (4) coffee jelly contributes minimal carbohydrate. Weighted composite estimate yields GI approximately 45. Glycemic load per 350 g serving = (45 × 10.8 × 3.5) / 100 = 17.0 g. gi_category set to low per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55 = low).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and high saturated fat from coconut milk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this beverage to occasional consumption due to moderate sodium (28 mg/100 g, approximately 98 mg per serving) and high saturated fat from coconut milk, which may contribute to endothelial dysfunction; choosing a smaller portion or requesting reduced condensed milk can lower both sodium and caloric load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar from condensed milk and moderate glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this beverage with caution as the condensed milk contributes approximately 9.5 g sugar per 100 g and the glycemic load per standard 350 g serving is estimated at 17 g, which is moderate; requesting reduced or no condensed milk and limiting portion size can significantly lower the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from coconut milk (6.3 g/100 g) and added sugar from condensed milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this beverage due to its high saturated fat content (6.3 g/100 g, predominantly from coconut milk medium-chain triglycerides) and significant added sugar from condensed milk, both of which may adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Coconut Cafe Latte with Condensed Milk and Coffee Jelly is a chilled coffee beverage originating from Singapore's cafe and kopitiam culture. It is assembled by combining brewed espresso or strong coffee with coconut milk, sweetened condensed milk, and homemade coffee jelly — cubes of set brewed coffee made with gelatin or agar — served over ice. The result is a layered, visually appealing drink with a rich, creamy coconut flavour, a pronounced coffee bitterness balanced by the sweetness of condensed milk, and a pleasantly chewy textural contrast from the coffee jelly. The beverage reflects the Southeast Asian tradition of pairing coffee with coconut milk and sweetened dairy, adapted into a modern cafe-style format. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 112 kcal per 100 g, with high saturated fat (6.3 g/100 g) from coconut milk and significant sugar (9.5 g/100 g) from condensed milk. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low), but the glycemic load per standard 350 g serving is moderate at approximately 17 g due to the volume of condensed milk. Clinically, this beverage warrants moderation for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease, primarily due to its added sugar and saturated fat content. It is not suitable for vegans due to condensed milk and is not dairy-free. It is generally considered halal and gluten-free.
common_user_queries: What is iced coconut cafe latte?
How many calories in coconut latte with condensed milk?
What is coffee jelly in a latte?
Is iced coconut latte high in sugar?
What is the GI of coconut milk coffee?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for this specific beverage exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted component blend of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for coconut milk, brewed espresso, sweetened condensed milk, and coffee jelly components. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for constituent ingredients. Medium confidence is appropriate given the estimation methodology and the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported for this beverage type in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimation. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in plant-based and dairy components at these proportions per USDA FoodData Central data. The food_name has been normalised from the promotional input 'NEW! Iced Coconut Cafe Latte' to the canonical form 'Iced Coconut Cafe Latte with Condensed Milk and Coffee Jelly' to remove marketing language and capture the full ingredient identity. Serving size of 350 g is estimated based on a standard large cafe cup; actual restaurant serving may vary. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per 100g × serving_g) / 100 = (45 × 10.8 × 3.5) / 100 = 17.0 g. GI set to 45 (low, ≤ 55 threshold) as an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this specific beverage is not available in approved sources.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Removed promotional prefix 'NEW!' from food_name and established canonical English name with full ingredient description.
Iteration 1: Built complete multilingual local_names for all five mandatory Singaporean languages (English via food_name, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Iteration 1: Estimated per_100g nutrients from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for coconut milk, espresso, condensed milk, and coffee jelly.
Iteration 1: Set GI to 45 (low) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed reference.
Iteration 1: Documented vitamin_d_mcg = -1 and selenium_mcg = -1 with explicit source-gap justification in notes (g3 compliance).
Iteration 1: Set all three ontology match_type values to 'closest_parent' using only allowed values (g5 compliance).

Iteration 2: Corrected the inconsistency in Q8 by replacing the incorrect traditional Chinese alias in the flat 'aliases' list with the verbatim string '椰香冰咖啡拿鐵（煉乳咖啡凍）' from 'aliases_by_language.chinese'. This ensures perfect consistency between the structured and flat alias lists, resolving the previous 'aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases' failure.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
